column1: Weekly sales performance review covering Jan 2025 to Feb 2026, focused on target variance, cumulative recovery, and weekday revenue structure.

column2: Sales underperformed target in the early part of 2025, with April 2025 emerging as the weakest full month.
Performance recovered strongly from June to September 2025, with August 2025 delivering the highest monthly sales and strongest positive variance.
Rolling variance moved from a negative position into sustained positive territory by mid 2025, indicating cumulative recovery against target.
Revenue is concentrated toward the end of the week, with Friday and Saturday producing the highest average daily sales, suggesting strong weekend dependence.
Early week demand appears comparatively weak and may represent an opportunity for targeted sales improvement.
End of week concentration suggests that operational disruptions on Friday or Saturday could have disproportionate revenue impact.
Rolling variance is a stronger measure of recovery than single month performance because it shows whether gains are cumulative.
The mid 2025 uplift appears to reflect a genuine improvement phase rather than random fluctuation.
